titel: Report on the implementation of the law on special control of certain foreign nationals, for the year 2025.
html:
In the letter, the government explains the application of the Act (2022:700) on special control of certain foreign nationals, from July 1, 2024, to June 30, 2025. The letter also describes the development of international terrorism and other serious threats to Sweden's security, as well as Sweden's participation in international efforts to combat terrorism.

During the relevant period, the government has made six decisions based on the Act. One of the decisions involved rejecting an appeal against the Swedish Migration Agency's decision to revoke a decision regarding a stay of proceedings, and another involved revoking a decision regarding a stay of proceedings at the request of the Swedish Security Service. Three of the decisions involved rejecting requests from individuals for a reconsideration of deportation decisions, and the sixth involved rejecting requests from individuals to overturn deportation decisions.
tldr: The government has published a report detailing how Sweden is working to protect the country against terrorism and other serious threats. The report outlines how the law on special controls for certain foreign nationals has been applied over the course of a year.
lattlast:
The government has published a report detailing how it has used a specific law to monitor certain foreign nationals. This law is used when there is a risk of threats to Sweden's security. The report covers the period from July 1, 2024, to June 30, 2025.

In the report, the government also describes the current state of terrorism worldwide. It outlines how Sweden contributes to international efforts to combat terrorism and other threats to the country.

During this year, the government made six decisions related to this law. These decisions include actions taken by the Swedish Security Service (Säkerhetspolisen) and the Swedish Migration Agency (Migrationsverket). Some decisions involved denying individuals the opportunity to appeal a deportation order.

In total, three individuals were denied a review of their deportation decisions. One decision involved the government refusing to halt a deportation, and another involved the government choosing not to halt a deportation at the request of the Swedish Security Service.
